title: ANA Ken Saro-Wiwa International Writers Residency
description: A residency in Port Harcourt, Nigeria (September 5-October 10, 2026) for practicing creative writers based in Africa who have published at least one work. Offers a transportation allowance ($200 for international residents) plus accommodation during the residency period.

title: Mandela Washington Fellowship for Young African Leaders (YALI)
description: The flagship program of the U.S. Young African Leaders Initiative: a 6-week academic/leadership institute at a U.S. university plus a Washington DC summit, for citizens and residents of Sub-Saharan Africa aged 25-35 (exceptional 21-24 year olds considered). Covers flights, housing, meals, and a small stipend. The next cohort's application window is typically announced several months in advance — check the official site.
visaNote: J-1 exchange visitor visa sponsored by the program.

title: Schwarzman Scholars
description: A fully funded one-year master's program at Tsinghua University in Beijing for outstanding young leaders aged 18-28, covering tuition, fees, room and board, round-trip travel to Beijing, in-country study tours, a laptop, health insurance, and a personal stipend.
visaNote: Chinese student visa sponsored as part of the program.

title: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
description: Full funding for a postgraduate degree at the University of Cambridge, for outstanding applicants from any country outside the UK. Covers the full cost of studies plus a £21,000/year maintenance allowance, one economy return airfare, and visa/health surcharge costs.
visaNote: Student visa costs and the Immigration Health Surcharge are covered by the scholarship.

title: Audley Travel Photography Competition 2026
description: Annual travel photography competition; the overall winner receives £10,000 toward a customized trip to Vietnam, and the runner-up £1,000 toward a trip of their choice. Entries close July 29, 2026.

title: Global Undergraduate Exchange Program (Global UGRAD)
description: Fully funded semester-long exchange at a U.S. college or university for undergraduate students from Kazakhstan, Mongolia, Uzbekistan, and other eligible countries, including coursework, community service, and cultural enrichment. Apply through your home country's U.S. Embassy Public Affairs Section or Fulbright Commission — deadlines and cycles vary by country, so there's no single fixed date.
org: U.S. Department of State (Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs)
visaNote: J-1 exchange visitor visa sponsored by the program.

title: YSEALI Professional Fellows Program
description: Fully funded U.S. State Department exchange placing emerging Southeast Asian professionals (ages 25-35) in 5-6 week fellowships with U.S. host organizations, followed by a Washington DC summit. Open to citizens of the eleven YSEALI countries, including the Philippines and Vietnam.
visaNote: J-1 exchange visitor visa sponsored by the program.

title: Global Korea Scholarship (GKS)
description: South Korea's flagship government scholarship, funding undergraduate, master's, and PhD study for international students from 150+ countries including Pakistan. Covers tuition, a monthly living stipend, airfare, and medical insurance. Application cycles are typically announced early each year for the following intake.
org: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), Government of South Korea
visaNote: D-2 or D-4 student visa sponsored as part of the scholarship.

title: Australia Awards Fellowships
description: Australian Government-funded short courses (2-52 weeks) in Australia for mid-career professionals from developing countries, covering travel and accommodation while building partnerships with Australian institutions. Runs in funding rounds; Round 22 is expected to open in the final quarter of 2026.
org: Australian Government Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT)
visaNote: Visa guidance provided as part of the award.
